title: KvCacheHandle sub-trait + KvCachePolicy types
goal: Define the KvCacheOps sub-trait reachable through KvCacheHandle: quantization level get/set, prefix-share commit/restore/evict, occupancy stats (bytes used/cap, prefix-cache hit rate), explicit eviction. Trait MUST be engine-agnostic so LlamaCppRuntime and CandleRuntime can implement against the same surface.
expected_diff_shape: New src/backend/handshake_core/src/model_runtime/kv_cache.rs defining KvCacheHandle + KvCacheOps trait + KvCachePolicy enum + KvCacheStats struct + KvPrefixHandle (opaque). mod.rs re-exports.
implementation_notes: Per spec §4.6.2 (AC-LLAMACPP-ADAPTER, AC-CANDLE-ADAPTER) and §4.7.1b (KV caching) the KvCacheHandle is the operator-controllable surface for KV cache behaviour. KvCacheOps trait methods: `fn quantization(&self) -> KvQuantSupport`; `fn set_quantization(&self, level: KvQuantSupport) -> Result<(), ModelRuntimeError>` (errors with CapabilityNotSupported if adapter does not support that level); `fn occupancy(&self) -> KvCacheStats`; `fn prefix_commit(&self, prefix_tokens: &[u32]) -> Result<KvPrefixHandle, ModelRuntimeError>` — commits a prefix and returns a handle that can be reused via GenerateRequest.kv_prefix_handle; `fn prefix_restore(&self, handle: &KvPrefixHandle) -> Result<(), ModelRuntimeError>`; `fn prefix_evict(&self, handle: KvPrefixHandle) -> Result<(), ModelRuntimeError>`; `fn evict_all(&self) -> Result<(), ModelRuntimeError>`. KvPrefixHandle wraps `(prefix_id: Uuid (v7 per HBR-INT-008), content_hash: [u8;32], token_count: u32)` — content_hash is a replay-resistant key derivation so a malicious caller cannot blind-replay another model's prefix; the adapter MUST validate handle.content_hash matches the on-cache stored hash before restore. KvCacheStats struct: `bytes_used: u64, bytes_capacity: u64, prefix_cache_entries: u32, prefix_cache_hit_count: u64, prefix_cache_miss_count: u64, quant_level_current: KvQuantSupport`. KvCachePolicy enum (consumed in LoadSpec): `Default { quant: KvQuantSupport, prefix_cache_ttl_seconds: u64, max_bytes: Option<u64> }`, `Disabled`, `Custom(Box<dyn KvCacheOps + Send + Sync>)`. The trait must be object-safe so `Box<dyn KvCacheOps>` is constructible. Subquadratic adapters in MT-114 will implement KvCacheOps with prefix-share semantics translated to SSM state-vector caching (the trait surface is preserved; semantics for SSMs use state vectors not KV pairs).
risk_if_missed: KV cache control (AC-INFER-LAB-8-TECHNIQUES technique b) has no operator-controllable trait; LlamaCppRuntime and CandleRuntime end up with bespoke surfaces.
validator_focus: Replay-resistant key derivation invariant: a tampered KvPrefixHandle (different content_hash) fails restore with a typed error; KvCacheOps is object-safe; engine-specific types do not appear in the trait surface.

minimum_controls: Replay-resistant key derivation negative test (tampered hash rejected).
All KvPrefixHandle Uuids minted via now_v7() per HBR-INT-008.
